Re
Fern PolrrrcAr- PRACTTcES C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916>322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
J:une 19,2002
Rhonda M. Fosbinder
Fosbinder & Fosbinder
415 Dairy Road, Suite E, #336
Kahului, tII96732
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-146
Dear Ms. Fosbinder
In response to your letter dated May 29,2002, in which you seek advice
concerning the status of the Entertainment Industry Development Corporation ("EIDC")
as a "local government agency" under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),I we are
unable to provide you with the assistance you seek.
The Commission's Legal Division provides written advice to persons with
questions about their duties under the Act. (Section 831 l4(b).) Under regulation 1,8329,
copy enclosed, the Fair Political Practices Commission is authorizedto provide advice
only to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Regulations 18329(b)(l),
18329(c)(1).) The Commission gives advice so that persons can ascertain their duties
under the Political Reform Act.
On the basis of the facts presented, it appears that the EIDC, or its authorized
representative, is the appropriate party to seek advice on the question of if it qualifies as a
"local government agency" under the Act. On these grounds, the Commission cannot
specifically comment on the status of EIDC about which you inquire. I am sorry that at
this time we cannot provide you with the guidance you requested.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appqr at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9- I 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. W-02-146
Page No. 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West, Staff Counsel
Legal Division
Enclosure
